# Autom8ion Lab — Full Reference

> Veteran-owned engineering firm building custom AI, automation, and software for compliance-driven industries: construction, healthcare, finance, real estate, US federal/state/local government, and the Defense Industrial Base.

This document expands the index at /llms.txt with full prose for the homepage, six industry pages, three priority service pages, and the capability statement. Last regenerated April 28, 2026.

---

## Homepage — Custom AI, Automation & Software for Compliance-Driven Industries

A veteran-owned engineering team building custom AI agents, workflow automation, and applications for construction, healthcare, finance, real estate, and US government clients. Security architecture and compliance documentation engineered from day one — not retrofitted.

Veteran-Owned · SDVOSB Certification Pending · Registered US Federal Contractor (UEI: YY2DR3KSENH7 · CAGE: 9YCS7).

### Built for industries where outcomes matter as much as compliance

We work with six industries that share a common need: custom-built technology that drives ROI without creating new compliance, security, or operational risks. Each industry gets an audience-specific service approach, not a templated one.

- Construction — project management automation, AI for takeoff and plan review, custom field apps, accounting and PM integrations for mid-market and specialty contractors.
- Healthcare & Life Sciences — HIPAA-compliant software, AI for clinical and operational workflows, EHR integrations, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 systems for health systems, payers, life sciences, and medical devices.
- Finance — custom platforms with SOC 2 and PCI-DSS controls, AI for underwriting and fraud, core banking integrations for banks, credit unions, fintech, and insurance carriers.
- Real Estate & Property Management — property management automation, FCRA-compliant tenant systems, lease lifecycle, HUD compliance for property managers, REITs, brokerages, and proptech.
- US Government (Federal, State & Local) — custom software, AI, and automation for civilian agencies, state government, and municipal IT. FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53, StateRAMP-aligned. Registered US federal contractor.
- Defense Industrial Base — CMMC 2.0 readiness and custom development for DoD primes and subs. November 2026 Phase 2 deadline approaching.

### When the software your team needs has to ship fast AND pass review

Most technology vendors force a choice. Off-the-shelf SaaS tools move fast but rarely fit your compliance, integration, or workflow requirements. Traditional integrators understand compliance but ship slowly and treat AI as an afterthought. We close that gap. Software and AI builders who design for HIPAA, SOC 2, CMMC, NIST 800-171, and FCRA from the first whiteboard sketch — backed by an in-house cybersecurity compliance arm and the federal contractor credentials to deliver in your environment.

### Three core services. One delivery model.

1. AI-Powered Enterprise Software & Applications — custom-built platforms, internal tools, mobile apps, and customer-facing applications with AI capabilities integrated where they create real leverage. Full-stack development with security architecture engineered from day one.
2. Workflow & Process Automation — custom workflow automation, AI agents, and process automation deployed where SaaS tools cannot go. We build for on-premises, private cloud, and FedRAMP-certified environments.
3. Cybersecurity Compliance & Engineering — CMMC readiness assessments and remediation. NIST 800-171 implementation. HIPAA technical safeguards. SOC 2 Type II preparation. We engineer compliant systems and document them for audit.

### A delivery process designed for outcomes and audit

- Phase 1 — Discovery & Scoping (1–2 weeks)
- Phase 2 — Architecture & Design (2–3 weeks)
- Phase 3 — Build, Validate & Document (scope-dependent)
- Phase 4 — Deployment & Ongoing Support (2 weeks + as-needed)

Most projects ship in 8–14 weeks. CMMC remediation runs 4–9 months. FedRAMP-aligned builds run 6+ months.

### Frameworks our work is engineered to satisfy

HIPAA / HITECH · SOC 2 Type II · FCRA · HUD (50059, MOR, EIV, REAC) · PCI-DSS · FFIEC · CMMC 2.0 (Levels 1–3) · NIST 800-171 Rev 2 · NIST 800-53 · FedRAMP Moderate / High · FDA 21 CFR Part 11 · Section 508.

### Engagement models for any procurement vehicle

We scope work to your contract structure — single-system task orders, multi-year IDIQ and BPA arrangements, fixed-price commercial engagements, T&M advisory work, or commercial MSAs. Registered with SAM.gov for federal, state, and local government procurement. Quotes are scope-driven and provided after a discovery call.

---

## Industry — Construction (/industries/construction)

AI, automation, and custom software for construction firms. Project management automation, AI-powered takeoff and plan review, custom field apps, and accounting/PM integrations for mid-market and specialty contractors.

We build project management automation that replaces manual reporting workflows, AI for plan review and RFI/submittal processing, custom field-service mobile apps for foremen and superintendents working in low-connectivity environments, integrations between accounting (Sage 300, Viewpoint, Foundation, ComputerEase) and project management (Procore, PlanGrid, Buildertrend, e-Builder) and field tools, document management for HUD-funded projects, OSHA documentation, lien waivers, and certified payroll, and bid generation automation with AI-assisted proposal writing.

Compliance considerations include HUD compliance for federally-funded construction, OSHA documentation, DBE/MBE/WBE reporting, E-Verify for federal contracts, Davis-Bacon Act prevailing wage documentation, state-level licensing and bonding, and lien waiver and notice requirements (state-by-state).

Buyer profile: mid-market to enterprise general contractors, specialty contractors, infrastructure firms in the $20M–$500M revenue range. The regional and specialty firms automating project workflows — not the Bechtels and Kiewits.

---

## Industry — Healthcare & Life Sciences (/industries/healthcare)

AI, automation, and custom software for healthcare and life sciences. HIPAA-compliant applications, AI for clinical and operational workflows, EHR integrations (Epic, Cerner/Oracle Health, Athenahealth, eClinicalWorks, NextGen), and FDA 21 CFR Part 11 validated systems for health systems, payers, providers, pharma, and medical device companies.

We build HIPAA-compliant custom software, AI for clinical documentation, prior authorization, and claims processing, EHR integrations, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 validated systems, patient-facing applications that pass IT security review, operations automation that does not violate PHI handling, and provider directory and credentialing automation.

Compliance considerations include HIPAA / HITECH (Privacy Rule, Security Rule, Breach Notification), HITRUST CSF, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 (electronic records, e-signatures), GxP frameworks for life sciences (GLP, GCP, GMP), state-level patient privacy laws (CA CMIA, TX, NY), and DEA EPCS for prescribing systems.

Buyer profile: mid-market health systems, ambulatory networks, specialty practices, behavioral health groups, payers, life sciences companies, medical device firms.

---

## Industry — Finance (/industries/finance)

AI, automation, and custom software for banks, fintech, and insurers. SOC 2 and PCI-DSS-aligned platforms, AI for underwriting and fraud detection, core banking and core insurance integrations for community banks, credit unions, fintech, and insurance carriers.

We build custom platforms with SOC 2 / PCI-DSS controls, AI for underwriting, fraud detection, claims processing, and document review, core banking and core insurance system integrations (Fiserv, Jack Henry, FIS, Guidewire, Duck Creek), compliance reporting automation (BSA, OFAC, CTR, SAR), customer-facing applications passing FFIEC scrutiny, AI tools designed not to trigger SR 11-7 model risk requirements, and loan origination and servicing automation.

Compliance considerations include SOC 2 Type II, PCI-DSS, NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 500, GLBA Safeguards Rule, FFIEC IT Examination Handbook, SR 11-7 (model risk management), BSA/AML and OFAC compliance, and state insurance department requirements.

Buyer profile: community and regional banks, credit unions, fintech companies, insurance carriers and MGAs, wealth management firms, payment processors, accounting firms with technology divisions.

---

## Industry — Real Estate & Property Management (/industries/real-estate-property)

AI, automation, and custom software for real estate and property management. Property management workflow automation, FCRA-compliant tenant screening, lease lifecycle automation, HUD compliance, and AI for document review — for property managers, REITs, brokerages, and proptech.

We build property management workflow automation, tenant screening systems with FCRA compliance (adverse action, consumer disclosure, dispute handling), lease lifecycle automation (renewal, escalation, compliance check), transaction management systems for brokerages, AI for document review (leases, contracts, HUD-50059, MOR/REAC documentation), custom platforms integrating with Yardi, RealPage, AppFolio, Buildium, MRI, Entrata, ResMan, affordable housing compliance automation (HUD, LIHTC, Section 8), and resident portals and applicant journey applications.

Compliance considerations include FCRA, Fair Housing Act (FHA), HUD multifamily and affordable housing requirements (HUD-50059, MOR, EIV, REAC), LIHTC, state-level landlord-tenant laws, ADA accessibility for tenant-facing systems, and state-by-state real estate licensing requirements.

Buyer profile: residential and commercial property management companies, REITs, real estate brokerages with technology operations, multifamily operators, commercial real estate investment firms, proptech companies, and affordable housing operators.

---

## Industry — US Federal, State & Local Government (/industries/government)

AI, automation, and custom software for US federal, state, and local government. Custom software, AI, and automation for civilian agencies, state government, and municipal IT teams. FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53, StateRAMP-aligned. Registered US federal contractor with SDVOSB pending status.

We build custom software for case management, citizen services, and internal operations, AI for backlog processing, document review, and intake automation, FedRAMP-aligned cloud services, Section 508 compliant applications, records management aligned with NARA / state retention schedules, modernization of legacy systems (mainframe interfaces, legacy database integration), and citizen-facing portals with accessibility built in.

Compliance considerations include FedRAMP Moderate / High, FISMA / NIST 800-53, NIST 800-171 (CUI), StateRAMP, Section 508, NARA records management, and state-level cybersecurity requirements.

SDVOSB note: certification is pending. Once approved, we will be eligible for SDVOSB set-aside competitions and SDVOSB sole-source awards under federal procurement preferences. We can currently compete openly and serve as an SDVOSB-track subcontractor under primes' subcontracting plans.

Buyer profile: US federal civilian agencies, state government IT, county and municipal IT, special districts, public utilities, large school districts, state universities, state DOTs.

---

## Industry — Defense Industrial Base (/industries/defense)

AI, automation, and software engineering for the Defense Industrial Base. CMMC 2.0 readiness, NIST 800-171 implementation, and custom software development for DoD primes and subcontractors. Veteran-owned. SDVOSB pending. Built for the contractors facing the November 2026 Phase 2 deadline.

We build CMMC 2.0 readiness (Levels 1–2, occasionally 3), NIST 800-171 controls implementation, custom software development that maintains CMMC posture, SSP (System Security Plan) and POA&M development, GCC High environment configuration and migration, AI capabilities deployable in CUI environments, and document automation for DoD reporting and submissions.

Compliance considerations include CMMC 2.0 (Levels 1–3), NIST 800-171 Rev 2, DFARS 252.204-7012 / 252.204-7021, ITAR / EAR (where applicable), and DCMA DIBCAC standards.

Important disclosure: We are not currently a CMMC Registered Practitioner Organization (RPO) or a C3PAO. We engineer systems to NIST 800-171 controls and produce the documentation your assessor will need. We work alongside RPO/C3PAO partners when independent assessment is required.

Buyer profile: DoD prime contractors and subcontractors of all sizes — from small specialty manufacturers to mid-tier system integrators. Especially small to mid-market subcontractors facing the November 2026 CMMC Phase 2 deadline.

---

## Service — AI-Powered Enterprise Software & Application Development (/solutions/software-development)

Custom AI-powered software, internal tools, mobile apps for compliance environments. Built for HIPAA, SOC 2, FedRAMP, and FCRA from day one.

What we actually build: internal operations platforms with role-based access and full audit logging; AI-powered document processing systems for regulated workflows; custom CRMs and case management systems; iOS and Android mobile applications built to pass corporate IT security review; customer-facing portals for compliance-heavy industries (patient portals, resident portals, vendor portals); AI agent applications operating inside private networks; API gateways and integration layers for systems where SaaS connectors do not exist.

Compliance considerations: security architecture decisions made at the start of the project — data classification, encryption strategy, access control model, audit logging requirements, environment segmentation. Compliance documentation produced in parallel with code: SSP drafts, control narratives, evidence collection.

We are not a body shop. We are not a generic dev agency. Software builders who design for HIPAA, SOC 2, CMMC, NIST 800-171, and FCRA from the first whiteboard sketch.

---

## Service — Workflow & Process Automation for Compliance Environments (/solutions/workflow-automation)

Custom workflow automation and AI agents deployed in HIPAA, SOC 2, CMMC, NIST 800-171, and FedRAMP environments. Auditable and audit-ready.

What we build: multi-step approval workflows with full audit trails; AI agents operating inside private networks with no external data transfer; custom integrations between systems where no SaaS connector exists; document processing pipelines (intake → classification → extraction → validation → routing); compliance reporting automation (BSA/AML, OFAC screening, CTR/SAR generation, EIV verification); internal RPA where commercial RPA tools are not approved; webhook and scheduled job orchestration in FedRAMP-bounded environments.

Who this is for: companies whose compliance posture rules out commercial automation SaaS. Federal contractors, DoD subs, healthcare entities with strict PHI boundaries, financial firms with regulator scrutiny.

Custom workflow automation gives you full control over the audit trail, data flow, and environment. The tradeoff is higher up-front cost. We help you decide which workflows are worth migrating off commercial SaaS.

---

## Service — Cybersecurity Compliance & Engineering (/solutions/cybersecurity)

CMMC readiness, NIST 800-171 implementation, HIPAA technical safeguards, SOC 2 prep. We engineer compliant systems and document them for audit.

We are software builders with an in-house cybersecurity compliance arm — not a security consulting firm and not an MSSP. We engineer compliant systems and document them for audit.

What we build: CMMC readiness assessments and remediation plans; NIST 800-171 controls implementation across access control, audit logging, configuration management, identification, incident response, and system integrity; HIPAA technical safeguards (encryption, audit controls, access management) implemented in code; SOC 2 Type II preparation including control design and evidence collection; FedRAMP-aligned environment design and System Security Plan drafts.

Important disclosure: We are not currently a CMMC Registered Practitioner Organization (RPO) or a C3PAO. We engineer systems to NIST 800-171 controls and produce the documentation your assessor will need. We work alongside RPO/C3PAO partners when independent assessment is required.

---

## Capability Statement

Autom8ion Lab — operated by Sitehues Media Inc. (DBA Autom8ion Lab). Veteran-owned engineering firm. SDVOSB certification pending. Registered US federal contractor.

- Legal Name: Sitehues Media Inc. (DBA Autom8ion Lab)
- Type: Veteran-Owned Small Business; SDVOSB certification pending
- DUNS: 05-289-2750
- UEI: YY2DR3KSENH7
- CAGE / NCAGE: 9YCS7
- Recommended NAICS primaries: 541511, 541512, 541519, 541611, 541690

Core competencies: AI-Powered Enterprise Software & Application Development; Workflow & Process Automation for Compliance Environments; Cybersecurity Compliance & Engineering (CMMC, NIST 800-171, HIPAA, SOC 2); Custom AI Agents & LLM Systems for Regulated Environments; Cloud Infrastructure for FedRAMP & GovCloud Workloads.

Differentiators: software builders with in-house cybersecurity compliance arm; engineered for audit from day one; veteran-owned (SDVOSB pending); multi-vertical experience across construction, healthcare, finance, real estate, government, and defense; founder-led with senior engineer access on every engagement.

Industries served: Construction; Healthcare & Life Sciences; Finance (banks, credit unions, fintech, insurance); Real Estate & Property Management; US Federal, State & Local Government; Defense Industrial Base (DoD primes and subcontractors).

Contact: Sean Richard, Founder · sean@autom8ionlab.com · +1 855-508-6062 · autom8ionlab.com.
